Title: changeCartConfiguredItem — Invalid sectionId (All-Zeros GUID) Rejected (VCST-4960)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Verify VCST-4960 fix: addItem(CFG_LAPTOP) with valid sections, then call changeCartConfiguredItem with a fabricated all-zeros sectionId (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000). Post-fix: validationErrors[] must be non-empty; original configurationItems must be UNCHANGED. Pre-fix behavior (3.1007.0): silently accepted, returned validationErrors: [], regenerated configItems. Fix shipped in XCart 3.1013.0 via PR #113/#110.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=reject_invalid] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=reject_invalid] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
[DATA label=reject_invalid] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.0.errorCode = "CONFIGURATION_SECTION_NOT_FOUND"
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems.length = 2
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_RAM_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_RAM_BASE_ID}}
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_STORAGE_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_STORAGE_BASE_ID}}
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] errorCode value in validationErrors.0 — look for INVALID_SECTION_ID or CONFIGURATION_SECTION_NOT_FOUND (exact code TBD from PR #113 source; record observed value in evidence for permanent documentation). read_back confirms original configItems unchanged after rejection.
Failure_Signals: validationErrors[] empty (fix not deployed or regression); configItems regenerated with bogus section (pre-fix behavior persists); errors[] non-empty at HTTP level (mutation should still HTTP-200); HTTP 500; read_back length != 2
References: xAPI; Mutation.changeCartConfiguredItem; VCST-4960; PR #113 (vc-module-x-cart) or PR #110; XCart 3.1013.0+; BL-CART-001

Title: changeCartConfiguredItem — Section Type Mismatch Rejected (VCST-4960)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Verify VCST-4960 fix covers type mismatch: addItem(CFG_LAPTOP) with valid Product sections, then call changeCartConfiguredItem providing the correct sectionId for RAM section but with type=Text (mismatch against the catalog definition). Post-fix: validationErrors[] non-empty. Original configItems preserved. NOTE: If validationErrors[] empty observed, classify NEEDS-INVESTIGATION (not FAIL) — type-mismatch validation may not be in scope for VCST-4960 fix; file follow-up to clarify.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=reject_type_mismatch] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=reject_type_mismatch] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
[DATA label=reject_type_mismatch] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.0.errorCode = "CONFIGURATION_SECTION_TYPE_MISMATCH"
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems.length = 2
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_RAM_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_RAM_BASE_ID}}
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_STORAGE_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_STORAGE_BASE_ID}}
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] RAM section catalog type is Product; sending type=Text with valid sectionId is a type mismatch. Post-fix validation must reject this. Record actual errorCode.
Failure_Signals: validationErrors[] empty (NEEDS-INVESTIGATION — may indicate type-mismatch not in VCST-4960 fix scope); configItems wiped/regenerated after rejection; HTTP 500; errors[] non-empty at transport level
References: xAPI; Mutation.changeCartConfiguredItem; VCST-4960; PR #113 (vc-module-x-cart) or PR #110; XCart 3.1013.0+

Title: changeCartConfiguredItem — State Preserved on Validation Failure (VCST-4960)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. State-integrity regression for VCST-4960 fix: addItem(CFG_LAPTOP) with UPGRADE options for both sections (different from BASE), then call changeCartConfiguredItem with all-zeros sectionId. Post-fix: both configItems must still reference the UPGRADE productIds after rejection — no regeneration from defaults, no reset to BASE. Tests that CartAggregate.ChangeCartConfiguredItem rolls back or does not commit the regeneration on validation error.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=reject_bogus] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=reject_bogus] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems.length = 2
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_RAM_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_RAM_UPGRADE_ID}}
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_STORAGE_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_STORAGE_UPGRADE_ID}}
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] UPGRADE productIds still present in configItems after rejection — confirms CartAggregate does NOT commit regeneration on validation error. extendedPrice from reject_bogus response items (if returned) should match UPGRADE_EXTENDED_PRICE, not BASE price.
Failure_Signals: configItems reset to BASE after rejection (regeneration committed despite validation error — pre-fix VCST-4960 behavior); configItems length changed from 2; read_back finds OPT_RAM_BASE_ID instead of UPGRADE (state clobbered); HTTP 500
References: xAPI; Mutation.changeCartConfiguredItem; VCST-4960; PR #113 (vc-module-x-cart) or PR #110; XCart 3.1013.0+; BL-CART-001

Title: changeCartConfiguredItem — Mixed Valid/Invalid Sections All-or-Nothing Rejection (VCST-4960)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Verify VCST-4960 fix enforces all-or-nothing atomicity: addItem(CFG_LAPTOP) with both sections at BASE. Call changeCartConfiguredItem with one valid sectionId (RAM section → UPGRADE option) AND one invalid sectionId (all-zeros). Entire mutation must be rejected — RAM section must NOT be partially updated to UPGRADE even though it was valid. configItems preserved at BASE for both sections.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=reject_mixed] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=reject_mixed] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
[DATA label=reject_mixed] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.0.errorCode = "CONFIGURATION_SECTION_NOT_FOUND"
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems.length = 2
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_RAM_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_RAM_BASE_ID}}
[DATA label=read_back] data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId={{SECTION_STORAGE_ID}}].productId = {{OPT_STORAGE_BASE_ID}}
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] RAM section stays at BASE despite having a valid entry in the payload — confirms all-or-nothing rejection. Partial-apply would show OPT_RAM_UPGRADE_ID in read_back which would be a bug.
Failure_Signals: RAM section updated to UPGRADE despite mixed payload (partial-apply — all-or-nothing broken); validationErrors[] empty (only invalid section silently skipped); read_back length != 2; HTTP 500
References: xAPI; Mutation.changeCartConfiguredItem; VCST-4960; PR #113 (vc-module-x-cart) or PR #110; XCart 3.1013.0+; BL-CART-001

Title: changeCartConfiguredItem — sectionId Format Variants All Rejected (EP, VCST-4960)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Equivalence partitioning across sectionId format variants: (1) all-zeros GUID, (2) random non-GUID string 'NOT-A-VALID-SECTION-ID'. Both are invalid sectionIds; both must produce validationErrors[] non-empty. Each call is a separate op; cleanup_pre before each. Verifies that the validation path is not tied specifically to the zero-GUID pattern but to any sectionId that does not match the product's catalog definition. NOTE: empty-string variant intentionally omitted from this case (covered conceptually by non-GUID pattern); add as separate op if author wants third variant.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item_a] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=reject_zeros] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=reject_zeros] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
[ERRORS label=add_item_b] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=reject_nonguid] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=reject_nonguid] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] All invalid sectionId formats (all-zeros GUID, non-GUID string) produce consistent validationErrors[] non-empty. Record errorCode for each variant to confirm identical error semantics.
Failure_Signals: Either variant returns validationErrors[] empty (validation tied to GUID-parse only, not catalog lookup); HTTP 500 on non-GUID string (format rejection before catalog check); inconsistent errorCode between variants
References: xAPI; Mutation.changeCartConfiguredItem; VCST-4960; PR #113 (vc-module-x-cart) or PR #110; XCart 3.1013.0+; EP technique (format-based partitioning)

Title: changeCartConfiguredItem Against Non-Configured LineItem — Clean Rejection (VCST-4960)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Cross-domain validation: add a regular (non-configurable) product to cart via @td(CFG_HOODIE.id) (confirmed non-configurable alias), capture its lineItemId, then call changeCartConfiguredItem referencing that lineItemId with any configurationSections payload. Verifies the validation is not limited to configurable products — the mutation must reject cleanly (validationErrors[] non-empty) without HTTP 500 or data corruption.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_plain] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_plain] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=change_on_plain] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=change_on_plain] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Non-configurable lineItem has no catalog configurationSections — any sectionId will fail catalog lookup. Mutation must reject gracefully. Record actual errorCode vs the configurable-product variant (may differ). NOTE: verify CFG_HOODIE alias resolves to a non-configurable product (was originally tagged for hoodie variant tests but suitable here).
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on non-configurable target (unhandled null product config); errors[] non-empty at transport level (GraphQL exception escaped); configurationItems populated unexpectedly on a plain item
References: xAPI; Mutation.changeCartConfiguredItem; VCST-4960; Cross-domain validation (050b4 scope)
